page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and network interfaces with a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard operations. The instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 14861.27 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 11469.52, both falling within the middle 50% tier. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring 1150.39, placing it in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance at 54.29 GB/sec for large blocks, but write performance for large blocks is weak at 24.73 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource profile, the instance is suited for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 10.85 GB/sec, and database operations, scoring 5002.27 on PassMark database tests. It also handles Redis SET operations at 12.97 million operations per second. However, it is less efficient for modern large language model inference, where a 7B model yields weak prompt processing speeds of 22.12 tokens per second. The older Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 hardware class offers a stable, dedicated resource pool but lacks newer instruction sets like AVX-512, making it a legacy general-purpose option.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving, though single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds are weak. It is suited for standard multi-threaded workloads, Redis caching, and web serving, but lacks modern instruction sets like AVX-512.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 6 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server with balanced memory-to-core allocation for standard multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 cores at 3.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core allocation
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Weak single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds
Suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose workloads
